bids.reasoning: I am targeting a maximum-utility opening bid of 1.00, since it is the very start of the negotiation and there is no time pressure to concede yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, though it includes issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, so I keep those where they are also good for me while holding my strongest preferences elsewhere.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself while making only a minimal concession from my prior maximum offer. The opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me, so there is no evidence of concession yet; I keep my strongest issues mostly intact and move issueA slightly from valueA to valueB as a small signal.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid repeating my last exact offer. The opponent’s valid offers have not improved for me and even worsened on issueD, so I should not make major concessions yet; I will concede slightly on low-weight issueF while holding my more important issueA/issueB/issueC positions.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers are not improving for me; in fact their latest bids lowered my utility by moving issueD from valueB to valueC. I am targeting a high-utility bid while keeping the concession on issueA and issueF from my earlier movement, and I avoid repeating my exact previous bid by switching issueD to an equally preferred value for me.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 while making only modest concessions. The opponent’s valid offers are consistently poor for me, mainly insisting on valueC for issues A/B/C/F, with only issueE aligned and issueD sometimes acceptable; therefore I keep my high-weight issues strong and use issueD=valueB plus issueF=valueB as limited flexibility without repeating my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, and the opponent’s valid offers have stayed essentially fixed and low for my utility, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than conceding much further. I keep my high-weight priorities on issueB and issueC, while offering issueD=valueB which costs me nothing and matches a value the opponent has repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a high-utility bid for me, about 0.944, since it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession. Their valid offers keep issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF at valueC and only vary issueD between valueB/valueC, so I will hold firm on my important issues while offering a small concession on issueF compared with my last bid.

At t=0.14 it is still early, and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded: their valid offers remain very low for me, mostly insisting on valueC for high-weight issues A/B/C and F. I am targeting a high-utility bid while using issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which match their repeated pattern and cost me little or nothing, plus a modest concession on lower-weight issueF.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession: their latest offer remains very low for me, especially on issues A, B, C, and F. I keep my high-weight priorities on issueB and issueC, concede somewhat on issueA and the lower-weight issueF, and keep issueD/issueE at values that are also consistent with parts of the opponent’s repeated offers.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility offer while making a visible concession on issueC, where the opponent just moved from valueC to valueB. Their offers are mostly firm around valueC on issueA/issueB/issueF and valueA on issueE, but their latest bid improved my utility, so I will test a reciprocal but still favorable tradeoff rather than concede on multiple high-weight issues.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 while making modest concessions on issueA and keeping mutually compatible values on issueD and issueE. The opponent has mostly held firm on low-utility-to-me offers, with only a small signal around issueC once, so I am not ready to concede major-weight issues further yet and I avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.90, since it is still early at t=0.22 and the opponent has shown little real concession in their offers. Their bids consistently ask for valueC on issueA, issueB, and issueF while keeping issueE at valueA, so I will concede the lower-weight issueF first while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.944 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.24 and the opponent has shown little real concession, repeatedly offering very low-value combinations for me. Their bids consistently favor issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which I can accommodate, while I hold firm on high-weight issues B and C and make only moderate concessions on issueA and issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.90, since it is only t=0.26 and the opponent’s valid offers have mostly stayed fixed rather than showing meaningful concession. Their repeated bids indicate they strongly prefer issueA/B/C/F at valueC while accepting issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, so I concede on the lower-weight issueF while holding the higher-weight issues close to my preferences and avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

At t=0.28 I am still targeting a high-utility bid rather than dropping to the opponent’s low offers, since their bids mostly remain around values that give me poor utility and show little real concession. I will keep my high-weight issues strong where possible, while offering concessions on issueA and issueF and keeping issueD/issueE aligned with values they repeatedly propose.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because time is still only 0.30, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has been very firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF but consistently offers issueE: valueA and often issueD: valueB, so I keep my high-weight issues mostly favorable while conceding the lower-weight issueF to their repeated valueC.

At t=0.32 I am still targeting a strong utility bid, but I can make a meaningful mid-game concession while staying well above reservation value. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC at valueC, but they once offered issueC=valueB and most recently improved issueF to valueB, so I am conceding on those more plausible tradeoff points while holding my high-value positions on issueB, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 because we are only at t=0.34, so I should not concede on the most important issues too much yet. The opponent has been very firm on issueA/issueB as valueC, but they have occasionally moved issueC to valueB and once issueF to valueB; their latest bid again shows issueC:valueB, so I will match that while also conceding low-weight issueF to valueC to improve acceptability.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.87 because we are only at t=0.36, so there is pressure to make a credible concession but not to drop near the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA/issueB as valueC, but has shown some movement on issueC and issueF, including their latest issueF: valueB, so I am matching that movement while holding my high-weight priorities where possible.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.824 while making a moderate mid-game concession because time is at 0.38. The opponent has held firm on issueA and issueB at valueC, but has occasionally moved on issueC and issueF, so I concede issueF to valueC and keep issueC at valueB while holding my most important positions on issueA/issueB away from valueC.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 while making a clearer mid-game concession. The opponent has been very firm on issueA/issueB as valueC, but has repeatedly accepted issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, and has occasionally moved on issueC or issueF; I will concede issueC to valueC while holding issueA/issueB at valueB and keeping favorable valueB/valueA choices elsewhere.

At t=0.42 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 while making a clearer concession than my last bid. The opponent has been very firm on issueA/issueB/issueC as valueC, but has consistently accepted issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, and recently moved issueF to valueB; I will concede issueC and issueF toward their pattern while holding the high-weight issueA and issueB at valueB.

At t=0.44 I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.87 while making concessions on lower/previously flexible areas rather than giving up the very costly issueB. The opponent has repeatedly held issueA/issueB/issueC at valueC while consistently allowing issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, with only small movement on issueC and issueF, so I offer a compromise with issueC and issueF at valueB while preserving my high-value issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, which is a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting my high-weight interests. The opponent has stayed very firm on issueA/issueB as valueC and usually wants issueC/F low for me, but they consistently accept issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, so I concede issueF to valueC and issueC to valueB while holding issueA and issueB at strong values.

At t=0.48 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.78: below my earlier high-utility bids but still well above reservation. The opponent has held firm on issueA/issueB=valueC while repeatedly offering issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, and sometimes signaling flexibility on issueF=valueB; with no messages, I trust only those bid patterns and concede mainly on issueC/F rather than fully yielding my high-weight issueA/issueB.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.82 while conceding on issues the opponent has repeatedly signaled, especially issueC/valueB and issueF/valueC. The opponent has held firm on issueA and issueB as valueC, but those are costly for me, so I keep valueB there while matching their acceptable pattern on issueD and issueE.

At t=0.52 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.684, conceding substantially from my earlier offers while still protecting issueB, one of my high-weight issues. The opponent has been very firm on issueA=valueC, issueB=valueC, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC, with some movement to issueC=valueB, so I align with most of their repeated pattern and keep only issueB at my best value.

I targeted a utility around 0.73, conceding on issueA and issueC while protecting the high-weight issueB and keeping issueD/issueE at values that are best for me. At t=0.54 we are just past mid-game, so I should make a meaningful but not deadline-level concession; the opponent’s recent offers consistently favor issueA=valueC, issueB=valueC, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and sometimes issueC=valueB or issueF=valueB, with no natural-language messages to add context.

At t=0.56 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making meaningful mid-game concessions on issues the opponent repeatedly demands. The opponent has been mostly firm on issueA=valueC, issueB=valueC, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC, with occasional movement on issueC=valueB; I will concede issueA and issueF while holding issueB and issueC for now because they are more valuable to me.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.73: I concede issueA to valueC because the opponent has been completely firm there, while holding issueB at my top value because it is high-weight and they have not shown any flexibility on it. The opponent has repeatedly kept issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which are also good for me, and they have occasionally offered issueC=valueB or issueF=valueB, so I use those as plausible tradeoffs rather than fully matching their low offers.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68 by conceding on issueA and issueF, which the opponent has consistently demanded, while holding issueB and issueD where my utility loss from conceding would be large. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, so I am moving closer to the opponent’s repeated pattern, and their offers show only limited concession mainly around issueC/valueB and issueF/valueB, with no natural-language messages to add other signals.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.524, which is a meaningful concession but still above reservation. Since we are past the midpoint and the opponent has remained very firm on issueA=valueC and issueB=valueC while occasionally offering issueC=valueB, I am matching the best valid opponent-side pattern they have signaled rather than holding out longer. There are no natural-language messages to weigh against the offer history.

At t=0.64, I am targeting a moderate utility bid rather than holding out for my earlier high-utility offers, since the opponent has remained very firm on issueA and issueB as valueC while repeatedly allowing issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA. Their offers sometimes move issueC or issueF to valueB, so I concede issueA and issueB but keep issueC=valueB and try issueF=valueB as a feasible compromise that improves my payoff over their latest offer.

At t=0.66, I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52 rather than holding out for my earlier high-utility bids, because the deadline is approaching and the opponent has stayed very firm on issueA and issueB. Their latest offer includes their recurring positions while conceding issueC to valueB, so I will mirror that valid bid to maximize the chance of agreement above reservation value.
